finding: Publish blockers are revealed one at a time in a tiny status chip rather than all at once

evidence.quote: Fix SEO title
Publish is gated behind a chain of readiness blockers that are revealed ONE AT A TIME in the status chip

persona_rationale: I have 10 minutes between customers; fixing one blocker, re-clicking, hunting for the next, and repeating burns my whole window before the page is live.

suggested_fix.current: Status chip shows a single blocker (e.g. 'Fix SEO title'), the next only appears after the first is resolved
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a single 'Before you can publish' checklist listing every remaining blocker at once, each row deep-linking to the field that fixes it

finding: The 'Fix SEO title' publish blocker contradicts the SEO title field's own helper text, which says the field can be left blank

evidence.quote: SEO title ... /placeholder: Leave blank to use page headline
'Fix SEO title' (shown even though the SEO title field says 'Leave blank to use page headline')

persona_rationale: Being told to fix a field the tool itself says I can leave blank makes me distrust every message the builder shows me.

suggested_fix.current: Blocker reads 'Fix SEO title' while the field helper reads 'Leave blank to use page headline'
suggested_fix.proposed: Don't block on a missing SEO title when it falls back to the headline; if the issue is the keyword, reword to 'Add your keyword to the headline or SEO title'

finding: A required 'Destination URL' publish blocker points at a field that is hidden inside a block-settings modal, not visible on the canvas

evidence.quote: the destination field is NOT on the canvas; it lives in Block actions → Edit settings modal ('CTA destination URL'). The blocker text gives no hint where the field is

persona_rationale: I'd hunt the visible page for a field that isn't there, run out of time, and give up before publishing.

suggested_fix.current: 'Fix content 1 · Destination URL' with the field only reachable via Block actions → Edit settings
suggested_fix.proposed: Surface the CTA destination field inline on the block, or make the blocker a button that opens the correct block-settings modal directly

finding: After saving a scheduled publish date the field re-renders empty and no 'Scheduled for…' status appears anywhere

evidence.quote: the field re-rendered EMPTY and no 'Scheduled for …' status appeared anywhere in the editor
Scheduled publish dd/mm/yyyy, --:-- --

persona_rationale: If I can't see that my schedule actually saved, I can't trust the tool to publish on its own while I'm serving customers, so I'd never use scheduling.

suggested_fix.current: Schedule field blanks after save with no confirmation
suggested_fix.proposed: After save, show a persistent banner 'Scheduled to publish {date} {time}' with a Cancel button, and reflect 'Scheduled' status in the editor header

finding: Schedule help text says publishing uses Pacific Time for what is an Australian business

evidence.quote: Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles). Leave blank unless this page should publish later.
Label says 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)' — for an Australian business

persona_rationale: I'd have to do US timezone maths to schedule a morning post for my own customers, and I'll get it wrong.

suggested_fix.proposed: Use the business's local Australian timezone (or let the admin pick) and show the resolved local publish time

finding: Saving a draft creates no restore point; revisions only appear after the page has been published

evidence.quote: revisions do NOT exist while drafting. Empty state: 'Revisions appear after publishing, library refreshes, or draft restores.' Saving a draft creates no restore point

persona_rationale: I work in interrupted bursts; the moment I most need an undo is when I come back and accidentally wreck a draft before I've ever published it.

suggested_fix.current: Restore points only exist after first publish
suggested_fix.proposed: Snapshot the draft on every save so a pre-publish mistake can be rolled back from the revision panel

finding: Created redirects have no delete or edit control, so a mistyped redirect is permanent from the admin UI

evidence.quote: Redirects table has no delete/edit controls — created redirects cannot be removed from this UI.

persona_rationale: If I fat-finger a path between customers, that mistake becomes a permanent redirect I have no way to undo from where I'm working.

suggested_fix.current: Redirects table is read-only (Old path / Destination / Status / Source / Created, no actions column)
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a delete action (with confirm) and an edit action to each redirect row

finding: The Readiness column in the pages list is coloured dots only, with no legend or labels

evidence.quote: READINESS column shows red/amber/blue dots with no labels
SEO readiness: Needs work / SEO readiness: Blocked / SEO readiness: Opportunities

persona_rationale: Colour-only dots make me anxious without telling me which page needs my attention first or what to do about it.

suggested_fix.current: Readiness shown as an unlabelled coloured dot per row
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a visible label or tooltip per dot ('Ready' / 'Needs work' / 'Blocked') and a small legend above the table

finding: Two parallel filter rows, the second using internal jargon ('Needs links', 'Updating', 'Metadata issues', 'Schedule failed')

evidence.quote: All metadata Needs review Updating Needs links Metadata issues Scheduled Schedule failed

persona_rationale: Seven extra jargon buttons I don't understand add mental load when all I want is to find my one page.

suggested_fix.current: Status filter row plus a separate workflow filter row with terms like 'Needs links' and 'Metadata issues'
suggested_fix.proposed: Collapse the workflow filters into one 'Filter' control with plain-language labels and tooltips explaining each

finding: The editor's SEO panel is an information dump: readiness chip, five form fields, eight scored rows, seven action items, plus media/governance/AI/comments sections

evidence.quote: Search visibility ... Search result ... Page content ... FAQ help ... Internal links ... Images ... Enquiries ... Trust proof ... Action Items ... Governance comments

persona_rationale: I want to write my page and publish it, not read a full SEO audit dashboard before I'm allowed to act.

suggested_fix.current: Editor defaults to showing the full SEO audit, action items, governance and comments panels
suggested_fix.proposed: Default to a minimal Title / content / Publish view and tuck the SEO audit behind a collapsible 'SEO score' section

finding: A 3-step wizard (13 clicks) must be completed before any content can be entered

evidence.quote: Clicks: 13 | Page loads: 3 ... 3-step choice gate (Resource → Blank page → Start building)
Step 1 of 3 ... What kind of page are you creating?

persona_rationale: Three screens of choices before I can type a single word is overhead for a quick job between customers.

suggested_fix.current: Mandatory 3-step page-type wizard before the editor opens
suggested_fix.proposed: Default to 'Resource page' and drop straight into the editor, with a one-line 'Change page type' link for the rare case it differs

finding: After the 'Changes published.' toast, the inline confirm-publish card stays open asking to publish again

evidence.quote: 'Changes published.' toast appears BUT the confirm card remains open asking to publish again ('This will replace the current live version…')
Confirm publish Publish this page? This will make this draft visible at /resources/ux-seo-review-test-1781071765035.

persona_rationale: I can't tell whether it actually published or whether I still need to click again, so I'd re-click and worry I double-published.

suggested_fix.current: Confirm-publish card persists after the success toast
suggested_fix.proposed: Dismiss the confirm card on success and replace it with 'Published ✓ — View live page'

finding: No 'Open live page' link is shown after publishing in the default panel state; the live URL had to be typed manually

evidence.quote: No 'Open live page' link was visible after publishing in the default panel state — the live URL had to be typed manually.

persona_rationale: After all that effort I want one click to see my page live, not to remember and type a URL.

suggested_fix.current: No live-page link surfaced after a successful publish
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a 'View live page' link in the post-publish success state
